meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Ship Faster
content.seo_description: Break free from basic app limitations. Master the SaaS starter kit workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver professional-grade apps fast.
content.answer_block: Stop manually configuring boilerplate. Use a high-performance SaaS starter kit to automate auth, database schema, and payment integration. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for iterative development, you transform from a manual coder into an assembly-line architect, cutting your development time by 70% per client project.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: 3 AM, staring at a blank terminal, manually setting up user authentication for the fiftieth time. The client is waiting, the deadline is breathing down your neck, and you’re stuck in the weeds of boilerplate code instead of delivering actual business value. It’s the invisible ceiling that keeps high-potential freelancers trapped in the cycle of low-margin, basic app builds.

But the game has changed. By treating your codebase as a product—a repeatable SaaS starter kit—you stop reinventing the wheel and start deploying scalable architectures in hours, not weeks. This isn't just about faster coding; it's about reclaiming your time and positioning yourself as the high-velocity architect your clients are desperate to hire.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Establish a production-ready repository structure that includes environment handling and essential middleware.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, production-ready SaaS starter repository using Next.js 15, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase. Include modular auth and billing patterns.

steps.title: Configure Identity and Access
steps.description: Implement enterprise-grade authentication so you never have to write auth logic from scratch again.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an authentication module using Clerk with role-based access control (RBAC) and protected layout wrappers.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'integrate clerk auth with middleware and protected routes'

steps.title: Database Schema Modeling
steps.description: Automate your database migrations to ensure consistency across staging and production environments.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a Prisma schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application including Users, Subscriptions, and Organizations tables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema with relations for multi-tenant saas'

steps.title: Stripe Integration Flow
steps.description: Connect payment webhooks and subscription tiers using a pre-configured checkout flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up Stripe webhooks for subscription lifecycle events and create a reusable pricing component.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement stripe webhook endpoint and subscription logic'

steps.title: Deploy to Edge
steps.description: Finalize the CI/CD pipeline to ensure your starter kit deploys globally with one command.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel configuration file optimized for edge functions and database connection pooling.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure vercel.json and optimize production build settings'

pro_tips: Always store your 'Golden Boilerplate' in a private GitHub template repository so you can spin up new projects with one click.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your auth logic immediately; it prevents 90% of production edge-case bugs.
Standardize your environment variables naming convention across all projects to ensure your deploy scripts never break.
Inject your own 'custom helpers' folder into the starter kit to carry your unique utility functions from project to project.
Always run a 'database reset' script before finalizing the starter kit to ensure the schema is pristine for the next client.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is live, create a 'Documentation' folder within the repo. Document your setup process so that you can hand off the codebase to a junior dev if your client load increases.
content.conversion_block: Stop building from scratch and start scaling your freelance revenue today. Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
